sources.label: Legrand 2026 regulated information (latest items: SRS Power acquisition May 20, Q1 results May 7 - nothing June 5-9)
Legrand datacenter acquisitions Keydak/TES (April 1, 2026 - prior news, already in thesis)
eToro LR.PA - Street targets cluster ~EUR 155-175, Hold consensus context

verdicts.rankRationale: No new evidence since June 6; the mild pullback (-1.36% today, roughly -2.7% on the week) with the busway/PDU thesis intact marginally improves entry asymmetry, but Legrand remains a $42B, well-covered name farther from the scarcest passive/power bottlenecks, so rank 28 in the secondary bucket stands.
verdicts.bull: Datacenter is now 26% of revenue and management guides the segment to the high end of 10-20% growth; four 2026 bolt-ons (Keydak, TES, SRS, ~EUR 285M added revenue) keep compounding the busway/PDU/rack franchise.
verdicts.neutral: Modest +8.1% YTD and a small weekly dip leave the setup unchanged; the exposure is real but priced as a quality compounder, not a hidden bottleneck.
verdicts.bear: 33 analysts cover it and the datacenter story is fully advertised; any hyperscaler capex digestion hits a consensus long with limited residual alpha at this rank tier.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: H1 2026 results (late July) showing datacenter order intake sustaining the raised segment guidance.
verdicts.primaryRisk: Crowding: AI-electrification is a consensus theme and Legrand is one of its most-owned European expressions.

verdicts.rankRationale: Hold at rank 83: nothing new since June 6, and the page's own thesis already concedes the market increasingly recognizes Legrand's data-center electrical exposure, so it is not getting more latent; a -1.36% drift day does not change centrality.
verdicts.bull: Power & grid centrality is structural - racks, busway, PDUs and building electrification all route through Legrand, and bolt-on M&A keeps widening the node.
verdicts.neutral: Exposure is intact but discovery is largely complete; the name belongs deep on a latency-ranked list, which rank 83 already reflects.
verdicts.bear: As recognition rises (26% of revenue from datacenters, sell-side targets up to EUR 175), the 'latent' premise erodes and the listing trends toward graduation rather than promotion.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: Further datacenter M&A or a marquee hyperscaler busway/PDU win that deepens network position.
verdicts.primaryRisk: Full recognition: once the exposure is consensus, the latent-node case expires even if the business performs.

verdicts.rankRationale: Hold at rank 3: yesterday's audit already balanced the electrical-distribution bottleneck exposure against the rerating, and with no regulated news since June 6 and only a -1.36% drift day there is no basis to re-rank within the power/cooling/electrical bucket.
verdicts.bull: Q1 sales +18% ex-FX with 20.7% adjusted operating margin, datacenter guidance lifted to the high end of 10-20%, and the SRS deal adds LV/MV power protection capacity directly in the bottleneck stack.
verdicts.neutral: Measured +8.1% YTD keeps valuation tension manageable; thesis tracking as written, no new datapoint either way since the last audit.
verdicts.bear: Margin guidance of 20.5-21.0% leaves little room for input-cost or integration slippage across four simultaneous datacenter acquisitions.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: H1 2026 results confirming datacenter growth at the high end of guidance and clean bolt-on integration.
verdicts.primaryRisk: Execution and pricing slippage in electrical distribution if datacenter order momentum cools into H2.
